mission.title: woodpecker-ci: Allow to change entrypoint / shell for ci step
mission.description: Allow to change entrypoint / shell for ci step. Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current woodpecker-ci deployment
steps.description:
Verify your woodpecker-ci version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
helm list -n woodpecker-ci 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working woodpecker-ci installation.

steps.title: Review woodpecker-ci configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant woodpecker-ci configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get configmap -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=woodpecker-ci
```
Currently `/bin/sh` is used as entrypoint for every ci step. If an image does not contain `/bin/sh` it can not be used with woodpecker ATM. For example `gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor:debug` only ships with `/bin/busybox`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow to change entrypoint / shell for ci step
steps.description: This feature requires changes to the Woodpecker CI pipeline backend to allow configuring the entrypoint and shell per step. See the source issue for community-proposed approaches and the latest status.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n woodpecker-ci -l app.kubernetes.io/name=woodpecker-ci
kubectl get events -n woodpecker-ci --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow to change entrypoint / shell for ci step" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This issue requests the ability to configure the entrypoint and shell for CI steps. Currently /bin/sh is hardcoded, preventing use of images without /bin/sh (e.g., kaniko). A configurable shell/entrypoint per step would resolve this.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with woodpecker-ci installed or the issue environment reproducible.
